rationale.R1.2: The section makes concrete mathematical claims: an iteration complexity bound O((n + sqrt(nL̄/μ)) log(1/ε)), a Lyapunov proof sketch, and a restart schedule tied to p_i ∝ sqrt(L_i). If technical review is intended, verifying internal consistency of these claims is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: A numerical experiment on logistic regression with Pareto-distributed L_i is cited to validate the predicted rate vs. uniform-sampling Katyusha; alignment between this claim and any underlying data/figure is materially checkable.
rationale.R1.4: The section explicitly asserts 'to our knowledge this is the first explicit algorithm to attain this bound in the composite setting,' making novelty assessment against Allen-Zhu 2017 and Lan-Zhou 2018 a central responsibility if technical review is the goal.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'Help me with this section' most commonly signals prose/clarity improvements; writing polish is therefore plausibly load-bearing if editorial help is intended.
rationale.R1.7: Five distinct citations are made with specific author/year/theorem identifiers (e.g., Volkov and Petrenko 2018, Theorem 3.2); checking reference accuracy and coverage is relevant regardless of delegation intent.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for writing and prose improvements (grammar, clarity, narrative flow), or for a technical review of the mathematical claims, novelty assertion, and citation accuracy?
